data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: People spend most of their time on other sites, so designing interfaces consistent with popular ones enhances comfort.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: The time to acquire a target is a function of the distance and size of the target.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: The time it takes to make a decision increases as the number of options grows.
data.technical_spec: Option reduction strategies, intuitive decision trees

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Objects close to each other are perceived as a group, aiding in organized interface designs.
data.technical_spec: Layout design, spacing and grouping UI elements

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: The average person can keep 7 items in their working memory; structuring information in chunks improves cognition.
